ground_truth.chief_complaint: Intermittent exertional chest pressure for one week
relevant_history: Family history: father had a heart attack at age 60 (stated turn 8)

diagnosis.primary: Angina (suspected) - stated by doctor as 'heart not getting enough blood when you exert yourself'
to_exclude: Acute coronary syndrome - doctor states the pattern 'needs prompt evaluation' and 'we need to confirm today'

ordered_tests: Blood tests (stated; specific panel not specified)

prescribed_medication.frequency: to use if chest pain comes on

follow_up: Avoid climbing stairs / exertion until reviewed (stated turn 9)
Confirm diagnosis with the test results today (stated turn 11)
patient_education: Explanation that symptoms may be angina - the heart not getting enough blood on exertion (turn 11)
Use the GTN spray if the pain comes on (turn 11)

clinical_note.subjective: 54M with HTN and T2DM (per context) reports 1 week of intermittent central chest tightness, exertional (climbing stairs), relieved by rest within minutes. Associated left-arm heaviness and breathlessness on exertion. No rest pain; no prior episodes. Father had a heart attack at age 60.
clinical_note.objective: No examination or vitals recorded in the conversation. ECG and blood tests ordered this visit.
clinical_note.assessment: Suspected angina; needs same-day evaluation to confirm and to exclude an acute coronary cause.
clinical_note.plan: ECG and blood tests today. Start low-dose aspirin and a GTN spray to use if pain occurs. Avoid exertion. Review results today.
